items.headline: Four regional powers converge on Pakistan to broker Iran war talks
items.body: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt met in Islamabad as Pakistan offers to host direct negotiations between Washington and Tehran. Pakistan has emerged as the diplomatic centre after traditional mediators Oman and Qatar came under Iranian fire during the monthlong conflict.

items.headline: European Commission confirms breach exposing hundreds of gigabytes from cloud platform
items.body: Attackers gained access to the Commission's Amazon Web Services environment, stealing over 350 gigabytes of data including databases and employee information. The breach affected websites hosted on Europa.eu, with threat actors claiming ongoing access to internal email servers.

items.headline: Jury finds social media giants liable in landmark addiction case
items.body: A Los Angeles jury ruled Meta and YouTube negligent for deliberately designing platforms to addict young users, awarding six million dollars in damages. The verdict validates a legal strategy targeting platform design rather than content, with thousands of similar cases now pending.

meanwhile.text: NCAA March Madness Sweet 16 tournament reaches final weekend with Duke, UConn advancing
Lab-grown oesophagus replaces full organ section in animals without immunosuppression drugs
Cold weather linked to 40,000 excess cardiovascular deaths annually in United States
meanwhile.search: cold weather heart disease mortality study

meanwhile.text: Younger American conservatives embrace traditional Christianity at higher rates than older generations
meanwhile.search: Generation Z Christian revival conservative politics

es.headline: Consumer prices jump to highest level since mid-2024 as war drives fuel costs sharply higher
es.body: Inflation accelerated to 3.3% in March from 2.3% in February, according to preliminary data released Friday. The increase was driven primarily by rising fuel and lubricant prices linked to the Iran conflict.

es.headline: Algeria agrees to boost gas supplies through Mediterranean pipeline as energy security concerns mount
es.body: Spain struck a deal to increase natural gas flows via the Medgaz pipeline during ministerial talks in Algiers. Algeria already supplies nearly a third of Spain's gas imports amid disruption from Middle East hostilities.

no.headline: Central bank holds rates but signals tightening ahead as inflation remains elevated
no.body: Norges Bank maintained its policy rate at four percent while indicating future increases may be necessary. Inflation has stayed above the two percent target, with officials citing persistent price pressures.

no.headline: Defense ministry announces major spending increase to meet alliance commitments
no.body: The government will add 115 billion kroner to defense budgets over the next decade. Opposition parties criticized delays to brigade expansion and air defense procurement in the plan.

uk.headline: Church of England installs first female leader in 1,400-year history
uk.body: Former nurse becomes 106th archbishop of Canterbury in ceremony attended by royalty and global Anglican leaders. The appointment drew opposition from conservative Anglican groups but marked historic milestone for the church.

uk.headline: Tens of thousands march through London in demonstration against far right
uk.body: Together Alliance rally backed by trade unions and civil society groups converges on Whitehall. Organisers claim half a million attended while police estimate 50,000 in what they call largest anti-far-right demonstration in British history.

nl.headline: Senate rejects law monitoring foreign donations to mosques and community groups
nl.body: Mayors and prosecutors will not gain authority to oversee foreign donations to mosques after the Senate rejected proposed legislation. Some coalition parties that backed the law in the lower house reversed course in the upper chamber.

nl.headline: Government settles with family of activist killed in political assassination case
nl.body: The Dutch government agreed to a settlement with relatives of an Iranian dissident murdered in The Hague in 2017. The family had argued authorities failed to protect him despite repeated requests for help.
